name: Link is descriptive
description: This rule checks that the accessible name of a link describes its purpose.
applicability_text:
This rule applies to any [inheriting semantic][] `link` for which all the following is true:

- the link is [included in the accessibility tree][]; and
- the link has a non-empty (`""`) [accessible name][].
expectations: Each test target has an [accessible name][] which describes its purpose.

examples.name: Passed Example 1
examples.description: This `a` element has an [accessible name][] from its text that describes its purpose.
examples.code:
See the description of this product.

This product consists of several web pages.

examples.name: Passed Example 2
examples.description: This `a` element has an [accessible name][] from the `alt` attribute on its `img` element that describes its purpose.
examples.code: This is the main content.

examples.name: Passed Example 3
examples.description: This `span` element has an [explicit role][] of `link` and an [accessible name][] from its text that describes its purpose.
examples.code:
See description of the product.

This product consists of several web pages.

examples.name: Passed Example 4
examples.description: This `a` element has an [accessible name][] from its `aria-labelledby` attribute that describes its purpose.

examples.name: Failed Example 1
examples.description: This link has an [accessible name][] which does not describe its purpose.
examples.code:
More

This product consists of several web pages.

examples.name: Failed Example 2
examples.description: This link has an [accessible name][] which does not describe its purpose.
examples.code:
More

This is the main content.

examples.name: Failed Example 3
examples.description: This link has an [accessible name][] which does not describe its purpose.
examples.code: This is the main content.

examples.name: Failed Example 4
examples.description: This link has an [accessible name][] which, alone, does not describe its purpose.
examples.code:
See the description of this product.

This product consists of several web pages.

examples.name: Failed Example 5
examples.description: These links have [accessible names][accessible name] which, alone, do not describe their purpose.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 1
examples.description: There is no [inheriting semantic][] `link` in this document.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 2
examples.description: This link is not [included in the accessibility tree][].

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 3
examples.description: There is no [inheriting semantic][] `link` in this document (`a` element without an `href` attribute do not have a role of `link`).

assumptions:
- This rule assumes that the purpose of the link is not ambiguous to users in general when seen in context on the web page, which is the exception mentioned in [Success Criterion 2.4.9 Link Purpose (Link Only)][sc249]. If the link is ambiguous to users in general, users of assistive technologies are not at a disadvantage when viewing the link out of context.
- This rule assumes that all [semantic][semantic role] `link` elements are used as links. An element marked up as a link, but that does not behave as a link would not fail [Success Criterion 2.4.9 Link Purpose (Link Only)][sc249].
accessibility_support: - Implementation of [Presentational Roles Conflict Resolution][] varies from one browser or assistive technology to another. Depending on this, some [semantic][semantic role] `link` elements can fail this rule with some technology but users of other technologies would not experience any accessibility issue.
